description_long: This object is a medium-sized bird with a long, slender, and slightly decurved beak, ideal for probing into crevices or flowers. Its body is elongated with a streamlined profile, and the plumage is predominantly a warm brown with subtle gradations and faint mottling along the back and wings. The tail is long and straight, tapering to a somewhat pointed end, and the wings are held close to the body, giving it a sleek appearance. The legs are short and dark, with visible claws gripping beneath the body. The head is smoothly contoured, with the beak extending well beyond the head's length, and the eyes are set back from the beak. The overall impression is of a bird adapted for foraging in dense foliage or on the ground, with cryptic coloration for camouflage.
description: A slender, brown bird with a long, curved beak and a pointed tail, featuring subtle mottled plumage and short, dark legs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bird, highlighting the long, curved beak and streamlined body.
description_view_1: Front view emphasizing the narrow head, beak curvature, and symmetry of the body and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the bird's elongated profile, tail, and beak from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the pointed tail and the back of the head, with the beak curving away from the viewer.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Subtle mottling and gradient brown tones across the body and wings, with a mostly solid color on the tail and head.
